title: V2 Records

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22V2+Records%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22V2+Records%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22V2+Records%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22V2+Records%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(February 2025)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(or **V2 Music** and **V2 Benelux**; V2 being an abbreviation for **Virgin 2**) is a [record label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label") that was purchased by [Universal Music Group](/wiki/Universal_Music_Group "Universal Music Group") in 2007, sold to [PIAS](/wiki/PIAS_Group "PIAS Group") in 2013[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) and partially acquired by Universal in 2022 (as part of larger PIAS 49% equity acquisition).[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2) In 2024, Universal acquired the remaining 51% of PIAS that it did not already own.[[3]](#cite_note-3) In [Benelux](/wiki/Benelux "Benelux"), V2 would operate separately from PIAS from 2013 to 2022, since the label was bought from Universal in 2007, henceforth Universal Music Group fully owns V2.

was set up by the **V2 Music Group** as a transnational marketing and distribution operation which licenses independent labels, as opposed to individual artist companies which is the standard industry practice, for release in Europe, Australia and Japan. This in-house licensing division has an exclusive international marketing team with representation in the UK, Ireland, France, Germany, Italy, Spain, Holland, Belgium, Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Australia and Japan.

was founded by Richard Branson in 1997 as part of the V2 International group with affiliates in USA, UK, Scandinavia, Germany, France, Italy and Benelux. In February 2007 the directors of V2 Benelux, Chris Boog and Tom Willinck, rounded off a successful management buy out together with their distributor, **Bertus Distribution** [[7]](#cite_note-7) part of the Artone Group. The Artone Group consists of V2 Records Benelux, Bertus Distribution, Townsend Music and Record Industry. V2 Records Benelux has offices in Netherlands, France, Germany and Belgium.

to **Sheridan Square Entertainment LLC** (SSE)[[8]](#cite_note-8) for $15 million. SSE then merged its label **[Artemis Records](/wiki/Artemis_Records "Artemis Records")** into V2 North America. The new label was effectively divested from the [Virgin Group](/wiki/Virgin_Group "Virgin Group"). Sheridan Square was later acquired by IndieBlu, which itself was acquired by [Entertainment One](/wiki/Entertainment_One "Entertainment One").[[9]](#cite_note-billboard-1-9)
["Universal Music Group completes 100% acquisition of [PIAS] Group"](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/universal-music-group-completes-acquisition-of-pias/). 15 October 2024.

["Kelly Jones on the history of Stereophonics"](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aCaWL00d73w). 27 October 2010. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20140523100809/http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aCaWL00d73w) from the original on 23 May 2014. Retrieved 13 August 2014 – via YouTube.

["Universal Music Group announces intent to acquire V2 Music Group, Ltd"](http://www.universalmusic.com/corporate/detail/488). London, England: Universal Music. 10 August 2007. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20130126044727/http://universalmusic.com/corporate/detail/488) from the original on 26 January 2013. Retrieved 13 November 2012.

Archived from [the original](http://www.musicweek.com/news/read/pias-buyout-of-co-op-cleared-by-ec-regulators/054100) on 4 July 2013. Retrieved 9 December 2018.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

**V2 Records** (or **V2 Music** and **V2 Benelux**; V2 being an abbreviation for **Virgin 2**) is a [record label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label") that was purchased by [Universal Music Group](/wiki/Universal_Music_Group "Universal Music Group") in 2007, sold to [PIAS](/wiki/PIAS_Group "PIAS Group") in 2013[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) and partially acquired by Universal in 2022 (as part of larger PIAS 49% equity acquisition).[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2) In 2024, Universal acquired the remaining 51% of PIAS that it did not already own.[[3]](#cite_note-3) In [Benelux](/wiki/Benelux "Benelux"), V2 would operate separately from PIAS from 2013 to 2022, since the label was bought from Universal in 2007, henceforth Universal Music Group fully owns V2.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=V2_Records&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The label was founded in 1996 by [Richard Branson](/wiki/Richard_Branson "Richard Branson"), five years after he sold [Virgin Records](/wiki/Virgin_Records "Virgin Records") to [EMI](/wiki/EMI "EMI"). V2 management was led by the same individuals that built Branson's balloon and the control position was held by a Canadian public corporation.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] The company was restructured after running into financial difficulties with Branson taking control and reinventing the brand.

The label was owned 95% by [Morgan Stanley](/wiki/Morgan_Stanley "Morgan Stanley"), the chief [financier](/wiki/Financier "Financier") of the company, and 5% by Branson.[*[when?](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Dates_and_numbers#Chronological_items "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers")*] Over the years V2 acquired [Gee Street Records](/wiki/Gee_Street_Records "Gee Street Records"), [Junior Boy's Own](/wiki/Junior_Boy%27s_Own "Junior Boy's Own"), [Blue Dog Records](/wiki/Blue_Dog_Records "Blue Dog Records"), and [Big Cat Records](/wiki/Big_Cat_Records "Big Cat Records"). The label also distributed many labels, such as [Wichita](/wiki/Wichita_Records "Wichita Records"), [Fania](/wiki/Fania_Records "Fania Records"), [Luaka Bop](/wiki/Luaka_Bop "Luaka Bop"), [City Slang](/wiki/City_Slang "City Slang") and [Modular](/wiki/Modular_Recordings "Modular Recordings"). [Stereophonics](/wiki/Stereophonics "Stereophonics") were the first band to sign to the label.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

V2 now operates in [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"), [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"), [France](/wiki/France "France"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"), [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), [Greece](/wiki/Greece "Greece"), [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands"), [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain"), [Ireland](/wiki/Ireland "Ireland"), [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden"), the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"), and the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] It was distributed in the US by [BMG](/wiki/Bertelsmann_Music_Group "Bertelsmann Music Group"); however it left for [WEA](/wiki/Warner_Music_Group "Warner Music Group") shortly after the formation of [Sony BMG](/wiki/Sony_BMG "Sony BMG"). Its headquarters were located at 14 East 4th Street in [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan"), the former US home of [Island Records](/wiki/Island_Records "Island Records"), which was in the same building as the former [Greenwich Village](/wiki/Greenwich_Village "Greenwich Village") branch of [Tower Records](/wiki/Tower_Records_(music_retailer) "Tower Records (music retailer)").

In April 2005, **[Cooperative Music](/wiki/Cooperative_Music "Cooperative Music")** was set up by the **V2 Music Group** as a transnational marketing and distribution operation which licenses independent labels, as opposed to individual artist companies which is the standard industry practice, for release in Europe, Australia and Japan. This in-house licensing division has an exclusive international marketing team with representation in the UK, Ireland, France, Germany, Italy, Spain, Holland, Belgium, Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Australia and Japan.

In May 2006, much of V2's catalogue, including multi-[platinum](/wiki/Platinum_disc "Platinum disc") artists [Moby](/wiki/Moby "Moby") and [The White Stripes](/wiki/The_White_Stripes "The White Stripes"), was added to [eMusic](/wiki/EMusic "EMusic"), making the label one of the most high-profile featured on the online music site, which mostly sells [DRM](/wiki/Digital_rights_management "Digital rights management")-free independent music at a cost considerably lower than many of its competitors.

In August 2007, V2 Music Group was sold for £7 million to [Universal Music Group](/wiki/Universal_Music_Group "Universal Music Group").[[5]](#cite_note-5) Subsequently, in October 2007, UMG partnered the US operations of its independent distribution arm [Fontana Distribution](/wiki/Fontana_Distribution "Fontana Distribution"), known as Fontana International, with London-based [Cooperative Music](/wiki/Cooperative_Music "Cooperative Music") of V2 Music. The intent was to enhance expansion through international agreements, and so independent labels from Europe under Cooperative Music would gain access to the independent American marketplace through [Fontana International](/wiki/Fontana_Distribution "Fontana Distribution")'s diverse connections in independent marketing, promotion and distribution support. Cooperative Music has had significant success with various acts such as [Fleet Foxes](/wiki/Fleet_Foxes "Fleet Foxes"), [Phoenix](/wiki/Phoenix_(French_band) "Phoenix (French band)") and [The Black Keys](/wiki/The_Black_Keys "The Black Keys").

In 2013, [PIAS] has acquired Cooperative Music (including V2) from Universal Music Group.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In November 2022, [Universal Music Group](/wiki/Universal_Music_Group "Universal Music Group") purchased a 49% stake in [PIAS] (including V2).[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)

### V2 Benelux

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=V2_Records&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: V2 Benelux")]

**V2 Benelux** was founded by Richard Branson in 1997 as part of the V2 International group with affiliates in USA, UK, Scandinavia, Germany, France, Italy and Benelux. In February 2007 the directors of V2 Benelux, Chris Boog and Tom Willinck, rounded off a successful management buy out together with their distributor, **Bertus Distribution** [[7]](#cite_note-7) part of the Artone Group. The Artone Group consists of V2 Records Benelux, Bertus Distribution, Townsend Music and Record Industry. V2 Records Benelux has offices in Netherlands, France, Germany and Belgium.

### V2 North America

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=V2_Records&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: V2 North America")]

In 2006 Branson sold **V2 North America** to **Sheridan Square Entertainment LLC** (SSE)[[8]](#cite_note-8) for $15 million. SSE then merged its label **[Artemis Records](/wiki/Artemis_Records "Artemis Records")** into V2 North America. The new label was effectively divested from the [Virgin Group](/wiki/Virgin_Group "Virgin Group"). Sheridan Square was later acquired by IndieBlu, which itself was acquired by [Entertainment One](/wiki/Entertainment_One "Entertainment One").[[9]](#cite_note-billboard-1-9)

On 12 January 2007, V2 North America announced that it was undergoing restructuring to focus on its back catalogue and digital distribution.[[10]](#cite_note-10) As a result, their employees were let go and their roster of artists left as free agents.

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=V2_Records&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: See also")]

* [List of record labels](/wiki/List_of_record_labels "List of record labels")
